input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## Using `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension to specify values for Light and Dark modes:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The binding reacts automatically when the user toggles their device theme — no event handling needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor` extension methods:

```csharp
public class MainPage : ContentPage
{
    public MainPage()
    {
        // SetAppThemeColor for Color properties
        this.SetAppThemeColor(BackgroundColorProperty,
            light: Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
            dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

        var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 24 };
        title.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
            light: Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
            dark:  Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

        var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
        button.SetAppThemeColor(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
            light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
            dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));

        // For non-color properties, use SetAppTheme<T>
        button.SetAppTheme<string>(Button.TextProperty, "Light Mode", "Dark Mode");

        Content = new VerticalStackLayout
        {
            Padding = 20, Spacing = 16,
            Children = { title, button }
        };
    }
}
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` | `.SetAppThemeColor()` / `.SetAppTheme<T>()` |
| Reacts live | ✅ | ✅ |
| Works with resources | Yes — reference `StaticResource` inside | Use `App.Current.Resources` lookup |

**Tip:** For larger apps, define theme colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them, rather than hardcoding hex values on every element:

```xml
<!-- In App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>

<!-- In your page -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```

This is a knowledge/explanation question rather than a code task on an existing repository. I'll provide a concise but complete example showing both the XAML approach with `AppThemeBinding` and the C# alternative.
